text: When you build a project that uses data from another service, you will likely work with APIs. An API connects your code to external servers using HTTP requests, such as GET to retrieve data or POST to send it. Most APIs return information in JSON format, which is easy to read and process. To access private data, you usually need a key for authentication. Remember to check rate limits, as many APIs restrict how many requests you can make in a given time. Finally, always include error handling in your code. An API may fail due to network issues or invalid data, so your program should respond gracefully.
